page: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-32 is an ARM-based general-purpose server instance belonging to the t2a family. It features 32 dedicated physical cores powered by the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture running at 3.0 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across most compute and memory workloads. The instance achieves a Passmark CPU score of 20,418.94 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 12,780.0. Memory bandwidth is measured at 165.27 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 76.68 GB/sec for large block write operations. Single-core performance is relatively weaker, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 1,086.0 and a weak single-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 8.63 GB/sec.

The server demonstrates strong capabilities in specific database operations, scoring 21,336.19 in Passmark database tests. It handles static web serving at 30.04 GB/sec and processes 40.8 million Redis SET operations per second. However, it exhibits poor performance in large language model text generation, particularly with 70B models where it drops to 2.31 tokens per second. This profile makes the t2a-standard-32 highly suitable for multi-threaded general-purpose workloads, web serving, and database operations, while being less optimal for single-threaded tasks or heavy LLM inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-32 is an ARM-based general-purpose server featuring 32 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture running at 3.0 GHz. Equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM (4.0 GB per core), this instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core capabilities, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 12,780.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 20,418.94, alongside a strong Passmark database operations score of 21,336.19. While single-core performance is weaker, the server excels in multi-threaded tasks, static web serving at 30.04 GB/sec, and Redis operations. It is best suited for scale-out workloads, web serving, and database applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-32 is an ARM-based general-purpose server with 32 dedicated Neoverse-N1 cores, 128 GB RAM, and strong database performance, suitable for multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-32 is an ARM-based server with 32 dedicated cores and 128 GB RAM, optimized for general-purpose and database workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered general-purpose instance featuring thirty-two dedicated cores and balanced memory for efficient multi-threaded scale-out workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 cores running at 3.0 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Strong database performance with 21,336.19 Passmark database score
Average multi-core performance with 12,780.0 Geekbench multi-core score
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded workloads
